meta.description: GTM strategy consultancy for technical companies entering Europe and NL-based businesses scaling EU-wide or globally.

brand.name: Bridgehead
brand.tagline: European go-to-market strategy for technical companies

hero.title: Your home-market playbook got you here. Europe plays by different rules.
hero.lead: We help technical companies win in European markets — where decision units, procurement norms, partner influence and trust dynamics don't behave the way your home market taught you.

items.title: Technical companies entering Europe
items.body: Product-led and sales-led scale-ups from the US, Israel, APAC and beyond whose proven GTM motion stalls on first contact with European buying reality: multi-stakeholder decision units, formal procurement, channel gatekeepers and slower trust-building.

items.title: NL-based businesses going EU-wide or global
items.body: Dutch technology and industrial companies that dominate their home market and now need a deliberate expansion strategy — because what works through your Dutch network does not automatically translate to DACH, France, the Nordics or beyond.

problem.title: Where home-market playbooks break
problem.lead: The product is rarely the problem. The gap is between how you learned to sell and how Europe buys.

items.title: Local decision units
items.body: The single-champion motion collapses when six to ten stakeholders — technical, commercial, legal, works council — each hold a veto and expect to be engaged on their own terms.

items.title: Procurement norms
items.body: Framework agreements, tender rules, data-residency clauses and payment terms differ by country. Treating procurement as a late-stage formality is the most expensive mistake we see.

items.title: Partner influence
items.body: In many European markets, integrators, resellers and trusted advisors decide which vendors are even considered. If you're not in their ecosystem, you're not in the deal.

items.title: Trust dynamics
items.body: References, local presence, language and long-term commitment signals outweigh velocity plays. European buyers reward vendors who prove they are here to stay.

services.title: What we do
services.lead: Focused engagements with a clear deliverable and a transfer of capability to your team — not a slide deck that dies in a drawer.

items.title: Market-entry diagnostic
items.body: Four weeks. We map your target markets' decision units, procurement landscape and partner ecosystems against your current motion, and tell you precisely where it will break and what to change.

items.title: GTM localization sprint
items.body: We rebuild the parts of your playbook that don't transfer: ICP and segmentation per market, message and proof architecture, and a sales process re-sequenced around local buying stages.

items.title: Partner & channel design
items.body: We identify the partners who actually shape deals in your category, design the commercial model, and build the enablement that makes the channel produce — not just sign.

items.title: Fractional GTM leadership
items.body: An experienced European GTM operator embedded with your team to run the expansion — from first-hire profiles to pipeline reviews — until it stands on its own.

cases.title: Cases
cases.lead: How technical companies made European markets work — anonymized, with the numbers that mattered.

newsletter.title: Newsletter
newsletter.name: The Decision Unit
newsletter.lead: A briefing on European B2B go-to-market: decision units, procurement, partners and trust — one practical idea per issue, no fluff.
newsletter.privacyNote: One email per issue. Unsubscribe any time. We never share your address.

contact.title: Talk to us
contact.lead: Tell us which markets you're targeting and where the current motion is stalling. We'll come back within two working days with an honest read on whether we can help.

footer.note: European GTM strategy for technical companies entering Europe and Dutch companies going global.
